name: boilerpy3

metadata.name: boilerpy3
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Python port of Boilerpipe, for HTML boilerplate removal and text extraction

documentation:
# BoilerPy3 ![build](https://github.com/jmriebold/BoilerPy3/workflows/Tests/badge.svg) ## About BoilerPy3 is a native Python [port](https://github.com/natural/java2python) of Christian Kohlschütter's [Boilerpipe](https://github.com/kohlschutter/boilerpipe) library, released under the Apache 2.0 Licence. This package is based on [sammyer's](https://github.com/sammyer) [BoilerPy](https://github.com/sammyer/BoilerPy), specifically [mercuree's](https://github.com/mercuree) [Python3-compatible fork](https://github.com/mercuree/BoilerPy). This fork updates the codebase to be more Pythonic (proper attribute access, docstrings, type-hinting, snake case, etc.) and make use Python 3.6 features (f-strings), in addition to switching testing frameworks from Unittest to PyTest. **Note**: This package is based on Boilerpipe 1.2 (at or before [this commit](https://github.com/kohlschutter/boilerpipe/tree/b0816590340f4317f500c64565b23beb4fb9a827)), as that's when the code was originally ported to Python. I experimented with updating the code to match Boilerpipe 1.3, however because it performed worse in my tests, I ultimately decided to leave it at 1.2-equivalent. ## Installation To install the latest version from PyPI, execute: ```shell pip install boilerpy3 ``` If you'd like to try out any unreleased features you can install directly from GitHub like so: ```shell pip install git+https://github.com/jmriebold/BoilerPy3 ``` ## Usage ### Text Extraction The top-level interfaces are the Extractors. Use the `get_content()` methods to extract the filtered text. ```python from boilerpy3 import extractors extractor = extractors.ArticleExtractor() # From a URL content = extractor.get_content_from_url('http://example.com/') # From a file content = extractor.get_content_from_file('tests/test.html') # From raw HTML content = extractor.get_content('

Example

') ``` ### Marked HTML Extraction To extract the HTML chunks containing filtered text, use the `get_marked_html()` methods. ```python from boilerpy3 import extractors extractor = extractors.ArticleExtractor() # From a URL content = extractor.get_marked_html_from_url('http://example.com/') # From a file content = extractor.get_marked_html_from_file('tests/test.html') # From raw HTML content = extractor.get_marked_html('

Example

') ``` ### Other Alternatively, use `get_doc()` to return a Boilerpipe document from which you can get more detailed information. ```python from boilerpy3 import extractors extractor = extractors.ArticleExtractor() doc = extractor.get_doc_from_url('http://example.com/') content = doc.content title = doc.title ``` ## Extractors All extractors have a `raise_on_failure` parameter (defaults to `True`). When set to `False`, the `Extractor` will handle exceptions raised during text extraction and return any text that was successfully extracted. Leaving this at the default setting may be useful if you want to fall back to another algorithm in the event of an error. ### DefaultExtractor Usually worse than ArticleExtractor, but simpler/no heuristics. A quite generic full-text extractor. ### ArticleExtractor A full-text extractor which is tuned towards news articles. In this scenario it achieves higher accuracy than DefaultExtractor. Works very well for most types of Article-like HTML. ### ArticleSentencesExtractor A full-text extractor which is tuned towards extracting sentences from news articles. ### LargestContentExtractor A full-text extractor which extracts the largest text component of a page. For news articles, it may perform better than the DefaultExtractor but usually worse than ArticleExtractor ### CanolaExtractor A full-text extractor trained on [krdwrd](http://krdwrd.org) [Canola](https://krdwrd.org/trac/attachment/wiki/Corpora/Canola/CANOLA.pdf). Works well with SimpleEstimator, too. ### KeepEverythingExtractor Dummy extractor which marks everything as content. Should return the input text. Use this to double-check that your problem is within a particular Extractor or somewhere else. ### NumWordsRulesExtractor A quite generic full-text extractor solely based upon the number of words per block (the current, the previous and the next block). ## Notes ### Getting Content from URLs While BoilerPy3 provides `extractor.*_from_url()` methods as a convenience, these are intended for testing only. For more robust functionality, in addition to full control over the request itself, it is strongly recommended to use the [Requests package](https://docs.python-requests.org/) instead, calling `extractor.get_content()` with the resulting HTML. ```python import requests from boilerpy3 import extractors extractor = extractors.ArticleExtractor() # Make request to URL resp = requests.get('http://example.com/') # Pass HTML to Extractor content = extractor.get_content(resp.text) ```

methods.name: boilerpy3.document.TextBlock.add_label
documentation:
Adds an arbitrary String label to this TextBlock.

```
param label

The label
```

methods.name: boilerpy3.document.TextBlock.has_label
documentation: Checks whether this TextBlock has the given label. ``` param label The label return true if this block is marked by the given label. ```

methods.name: boilerpy3.document.TextBlock.add_labels
documentation: Adds a set of labels to this TextBlock. \null\-references are silently ignored. ``` param labels The labels to be added. ```

Describes a block of text. A block can be an "atomic" text element (i.e., a sequence of text that is not interrupted
by any HTML markup) or a compound of such atomic elements.

classes.name: boilerpy3.extractors.Extractor

methods.name: boilerpy3.extractors.Extractor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize extractor

```
param filtr

filter

param raise_on_failure

whether or not to raise an exception if a text extraction failure is encountered.

param request_kwargs

kwargs to pass to urllib.request
```

The base class of Extractors. Also provides some helper methods to quickly retrieve the text that remained after
processing.

classes.name: boilerpy3.extractors.DefaultExtractor

methods.name: boilerpy3.extractors.DefaultExtractor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize extractor

```
param raise_on_failure

whether or not to raise an exception if a text extraction failure is encountered.

param request_kwargs

kwargs to pass to urllib.request
```
Usually worse than ArticleExtractor, but simpler/no heuristics. A quite generic full-text extractor.

classes.name: boilerpy3.extractors.ArticleExtractor

methods.name: boilerpy3.extractors.ArticleExtractor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize extractor

```
param raise_on_failure

whether or not to raise an exception if a text extraction failure is encountered.

param request_kwargs

kwargs to pass to urllib.request
```

A full-text extractor which is tuned towards news articles. In this scenario it achieves higher accuracy than
DefaultExtractor. Works very well for most types of Article-like HTML.

classes.name: boilerpy3.extractors.LargestContentExtractor

methods.name: boilerpy3.extractors.LargestContentExtractor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize extractor

```
param raise_on_failure

whether or not to raise an exception if a text extraction failure is encountered.

param request_kwargs

kwargs to pass to urllib.request
```

A full-text extractor which extracts the largest text component of a page. For news articles, it may perform better
than the DefaultExtractor, but usually worse than ArticleExtractor. Like DefaultExtractor, but keeps the largest
text block only.

classes.name: boilerpy3.extractors.CanolaExtractor

documentation:
Initialize extractor

```
param raise_on_failure

whether or not to raise an exception if a text extraction failure is encountered.

param request_kwargs

kwargs to pass to urllib.request
```
Trained on krdwrd Canola (different definition of "boilerplate"). You may give it a try.

documentation:
Initialize extractor

```
param raise_on_failure

whether or not to raise an exception if a text extraction failure is encountered.

param request_kwargs

kwargs to pass to urllib.request
```

Marks everything as content. Dummy Extractor; should return the input text. Use this to double-check that your
problem is within a particular BoilerpipeExtractor, or somewhere else.

A full-text extractor which is tuned towards extracting sentences from news articles.

classes.name: boilerpy3.extractors.KeepEverythingWithMinKWordsFilter
documentation:
A full-text extractor which extracts the largest text component of a page. For news articles, it may perform better
than the DefaultExtractor, but usually worse than ArticleExtractor.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.BoilerpipeFilter

methods.name: boilerpy3.filters.BoilerpipeFilter.subtract_blocks
documentation:
inefficient but in place: for block in blocksToRemove: blockArr.remove(blocksToRemove) efficiently subtracts
second array from first assuming blocksToRemove shows up in the same order as blocArr
Boilerpipe abstract interface

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.FilterChain
documentation: Chain together multiple filters in sequence

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.MarkEverythingContentFilter
documentation: Marks all blocks as content.
SIMPLE FILTERS

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.InvertedFilter
documentation: Reverts the "is_content" flag for all TextBlocks

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.BoilerplateBlockFilter
documentation: Removes TextBlocks which have explicitly been marked as "not content".

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.MinWordsFilter
documentation: Keeps only those content blocks which contain at least k words.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.MinClauseWordsFilter
documentation: Keeps only blocks that have at least one segment fragment ("clause") with at least k words (default: 5). NOTE: You might consider using the SplitParagraphBlocksFilter upstream. See SplitParagraphBlocksFilter

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.SplitParagraphBlocksFilter
documentation:
Splits TextBlocks at paragraph boundaries.

NOTE: This is not fully supported (i.e., it will break highlighting support via #getContainedTextElements()), but
this one probably is necessary for some other filters.

See MinClauseWordsFilter

methods.name: boilerpy3.filters.SurroundingToContentFilter.__init__
documentation:
this is now default when no arguments are passed

INSTANCE_TEXT = SurroundingToContentFilter(TextBlockCondition())

ctor - condition is an function for an additional condition to determine if it can be made content

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.LabelToBoilerplateFilter
documentation:
Marks all blocks that contain a given label as "boilerplate".

INSTANCE_STRICTLY_NOT_CONTENT = LabelToBoilerplateFilter(DefaultLabels.STRICTLY_NOT_CONTENT)

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.LabelToContentFilter
documentation: Marks all blocks that contain a given label as "content".

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.SimpleBlockFusionProcessor
documentation: Merges two subsequent blocks if their text densities are equal.
GENERIC HEURISTIC FILTERS

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.LabelFusion

methods.name: boilerpy3.filters.LabelFusion.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new LabelFusion instance.

```
param label_prefix

The maximum distance in blocks.
```
Fuses adjacent blocks if their labels are equal.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.BlockProximityFusion

methods.name: boilerpy3.filters.BlockProximityFusion.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new BlockProximityFusion instance.

```
param max_blocks_distance

The maximum distance in blocks.

param content_only



param same_tag_level_only

```

Fuses adjacent blocks if their distance (in blocks) does not exceed a certain limit. This probably makes sense only
in cases where an upstream filter already has removed some blocks.

MAX_DISTANCE_1 = BlockProximityFusion(1, False, False)
MAX_DISTANCE_1_SAME_TAGLEVEL = BlockProximityFusion(1, False, True)
MAX_DISTANCE_1_CONTENT_ONLY = BlockProximityFusion(1, True, False)
MAX_DISTANCE_1_CONTENT_ONLY_SAME_TAGLEVEL = BlockProximityFusion(1, True, True)

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.KeepLargestBlockFilter
documentation:
Keeps the largest TextBlock only (by the number of words). In case of more than one block with the same
number of words, the first block is chosen. All discarded blocks are marked "not content" and flagged as
DefaultLabels.

Note that, by default, only TextBlocks marked as "content" are taken into consideration.

INSTANCE = KeepLargestBlockFilter(False)
INSTANCE_EXPAND_TO_SAME_TAGLEVEL = KeepLargestBlockFilter(True)

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.ExpandTitleToContentFilter
documentation:
Marks all TextBlocks "content" which are between the headline and the part that has already been marked
content, if they are marked DefaultLabels#MIGHT_BE_CONTENT.

This filter is quite specific to the news domain.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.AddPrecedingLabelsFilter

methods.name: boilerpy3.filters.AddPrecedingLabelsFilter.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new AddPrecedingLabelsFilter instance.

INSTANCE = AddPrecedingLabelsFilter("")
INSTANCE_PRE = AddPrecedingLabelsFilter("^")
Adds the labels of the preceding block to the current block, optionally adding a prefix.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.DocumentTitleMatchClassifier
documentation: Marks TextBlocks which contain parts of the HTML <TITLE> tag, using some heuristics which are quite specific to the news domain.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.HeuristicFilterBase
documentation: Base class for some heuristics that are used by boilerpipe filters.
ENGLISH HEURISTIC FILTERS
--- Heuristic Filters that have been trained on English laguage text

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.MinFulltextWordsFilter
documentation:
Keeps only those content blocks which contain at least k full-text words (measured by
HeuristicFilterBase#get_num_full_text_words(TextBlock). k is 30 by default.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.KeepLargestFulltextBlockFilter
documentation:
Keeps the largest TextBlock only (by the number of words). In case of more than one block with the same
number of words, the first block is chosen. All discarded blocks are marked "not content" and flagged as
DefaultLabels. As opposed to KeepLargestBlockFilter, the number of words are computed using HeuristicFilterBase
get_num_full_text_words(TextBlock), which only counts words that occur in text elements with at least 9 words and
are thus believed to be full text.

NOTE: Without language-specific fine-tuning (i.e., running the default instance), this filter may lead to suboptimal
results. You better use KeepLargestBlockFilter instead, which works at the level of number-of-words instead
of text densities.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.IgnoreBlocksAfterContentFilter

methods.name: boilerpy3.filters.IgnoreBlocksAfterContentFilter.__init__
documentation:
DEFAULT_INSTANCE = IgnoreBlocksAfterContentFilter(60)
INSTANCE_200 = IgnoreBlocksAfterContentFilter(200)

Marks all blocks as "non-content" that occur after blocks that have been marked DefaultLabels#INDICATES_END_OF_TEXT.
These marks are ignored unless a minimum number of words in content blocks occur before this mark (default: 60).
This can be used in conjunction with an upstream TerminatingBlocksFinder.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.IgnoreBlocksAfterContentFromEndFilter
documentation:
Marks all blocks as "non-content" that occur after blocks that have been marked DefaultLabels#INDICATES_END_OF_TEXT,
and after any content block. This filter can be used in conjunction with an upstream TerminatingBlocksFinder.

See TerminatingBlocksFinder

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.TerminatingBlocksFinder

methods.name: boilerpy3.filters.TerminatingBlocksFinder.starts_with_number
documentation:
Checks whether the given text t starts with a sequence of digits, followed by one of the given strings.

```
param text

The text to examine

param match_str_arr

Any strings that may follow the digits.

return

true if at least one combination matches
```

Finds blocks which are potentially indicating the end of an article text and marks them with
DefaultLabels#INDICATES_END_OF_TEXT. This can be used in conjunction with a downstream
IgnoreBlocksAfterContentFilter.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.NumWordsRulesClassifier
documentation:
Classifies TextBlocks as content/not-content through rules that have been determined using the C4.8 machine
learning algorithm, as described in the paper "Boilerplate Detection using Shallow Text Features" (WSDM 2010),
particularly using number of words per block and link density per block.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.DensityRulesClassifier
documentation:
Classifies TextBlocks as content/not-content through rules that have been determined using the C4.8 machine learning
algorithm, as described in the paper "Boilerplate Detection using Shallow Text Features", particularly using text
densities and link densities.

classes.name: boilerpy3.filters.CanolaFilter
documentation:
A full-text extractor trained on http://krdwrd.org/,
https://krdwrd.org/trac/attachment/wiki/Corpora/Canola/CANOLA.pdf. Works well with SimpleEstimator, too.

modules.name: boilerpy3.parser
documentation:
This file is licensed under the terms of the Apache License, Version 2.0. See the LICENSE file in the root of this
repository for complete details.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.TagAction
documentation: TAG ACTIONS

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.IgnorableElementTagAction
documentation: Marks this tag as "ignorable", i.e. all its inner content is silently skipped.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.AnchorTextTagAction
documentation: Marks this tag as "anchor" (this should usually only be set for the <A> tag). Anchor tags may not be nested. There is a bug in certain versions of NekoHTML which still allows nested tags. If boilerpipe encounters such nestings, a SAXException is thrown.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.BodyTagAction
documentation: Marks this tag the body element (this should usually only be set for the <BODY> tag).

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.InlineWhitespaceTagAction
documentation: Marks this tag a simple "inline" element, which generates whitespace, but no new block.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.InlineTagAction
documentation: Marks this tag a simple "inline" element, which neither generates whitespace, nor a new block.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.BlockTagAction
documentation: Explicitly marks this tag a simple "block-level" element, which always generates whitespace

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.FontTagAction
documentation: Special TagAction for the <FONT> tag, which keeps track of the absolute and relative font size.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.InlineTagLabelAction
documentation: CommonTagActions for inline elements, which triggers some LabelAction on the generated TextBlock.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.BlockTagLabelAction
documentation: CommonTagActions for block-level elements, which triggers some LabelAction} on the generated TextBlock.

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.LabelAction
documentation: Helps adding labels to TextBlocks.
LABEL ACTIONS

classes.name: boilerpy3.parser.BoilerpipeBaseParser

methods.name: boilerpy3.parser.BoilerpipeBaseParser.__init__
documentation:
Constructs a BoilerpipeHTMLContentHandler using the given TagActionMap.

```
param tag_actions

The TagActionMap to use, e.g. DefaultTagActionMap.
```

methods.name: boilerpy3.parser.BoilerpipeBaseParser.recycle
documentation: Recycles this instance.

methods.name: boilerpy3.parser.BoilerpipeBaseParser.to_text_document
documentation:
Returns a TextDocument containing the extracted TextBlocks. NOTE: Only call this after parsing.

```
return

The TextDocument
```

A simple SAX ContentHandler, used by BoilerpipeSAXInput. Can be used by different parser
implementations, e.g. NekoHTML and TagSoup.
SAX CONTENT HANDLER
